activation_results.reason: Input matches the description verbatim — canonical supply chain audit across all listed ecosystems.
Review is a natural synonym of audit and the ecosystem list is identical, so the skill should fire.
User invokes the skill by exact name, which is an unambiguous activation signal.
SSH key forwarding on Windows is unrelated to supply chain auditing.
Meta question about what skills are has no overlap with supply chain audit scope.

criterion_results.text: Australian English used throughout the narrative (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise)
criterion_results.evidence: Narrative uses neutral/AU-compatible spellings; no US-only spellings detected (e.g., no 'color', 'optimize', 'behavior', 'organize').

criterion_results.text: Findings cite file:line evidence and are categorised by severity
criterion_results.evidence: Findings reference files (e.g., `charts/api/values.yaml`, `modules/vpc`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`) and are categorised by severity (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM), but no explicit line numbers are cited.
Report contains Executive Summary, Category Scores, Per-Ecosystem Findings, Repo-wide Findings, SLSA Self-Assessment, Vulnerability Scan Summary, Risk Register, Action Batches, Suppressed.

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)
criterion_results.evidence: Title names target `acme-api`; concrete paths used throughout (`package-lock.json`, `charts/api/values.yaml`, `modules/vpc`, `modules/rds`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`).

judge_result.notes: The artefact is a well-structured supply chain audit for `acme-api` covering the canonical sections, severity-graded findings, SLSA gap analysis, and a risk register with remediation. Australian English is consistent and no placeholder paths appear. The one shortfall against the stated criteria is the absence of explicit line-number citations alongside file references; findings name files and packages but stop at file-level granularity rather than file:line. Criterion 3's canonical-sections list is truncated in the prompt, so it was assessed against the visible expectation that standard audit sections appear, which they do.
